summary: The no-unit-tests decision for Task 6 is well-justified under user-spec D8 (Phase 1 writes tests only for the allowlist parser) and consistent with the Task-5 precedent accepted at round-level `passed`. DefaultModelRegistry's concurrency, GPU->CPU fallback, and stale-instance guard are explicit Phase-2 test debt per the tech-spec test-reviewer low finding; structural AC anchors (lifecycleMutex.withLock count, `currentInstance === model.instance` literal, `callbackFlow` presence, `inference-run`/`allowlist` absence) are already enforced by smoke greps. The implementation is not materially harder to unit-test later: `LlmModelHelper` is already interface-injected (mockable), the Mutex is an internal impl detail, and `ErrorLog`/`AllowlistLoader`/`DownloadRepository` are ctor-injected abstractions — so Phase-2 can add JUnit5+MockK without refactor. Two minor observations on test-debt clarity and a nit on verification symmetry; none block approval.

findings.issue: Phase-2 test-debt inventory is implicit — the task references `test-reviewer low finding on tech-spec` and `Phase 2 testing debt` but does not enumerate the exact behaviours that must be covered when the JUnit5+MockK infra lands (Mutex serialisation of initialize/cleanup, stale-instance abort path, GPU->CPU fallback with cleanUp-before-retry, `stopResponse` before `cleanUp` on in-flight inference, callbackFlow emission forwarding, startup scan resilience). Without an explicit list, Phase-2 may miss one of the three behavioural ACs marked review-only (`repeat-initialize after cleanup`, `parallel initialize/cleanup serialised`, `double cleanup is safe no-op`).
findings.recommendation: In decisions.md for Task 6, add a short `test_debt_phase2` section that lists the behavioural ACs verbatim and maps each to a recommended test name (e.g. `initialize_retriesOnCpu_whenGpuFails`, `cleanup_isNoOp_whenInstanceNull`, `cleanup_abortsClose_whenInstanceSwapped`, `parallelInitializeCleanup_serialisedByMutex`, `resumePartialDownloads_reenqueuesGallerytmp`). This turns the deferred debt into a ready-to-execute Phase-2 ticket without re-deriving the test matrix from the code.

findings.issue: Smoke greps cover structural anchors but not the `stopResponse(model)` ordering guarantee (AC says `stopResponse` must precede `cleanUp(model, onDone = { })` in the release path). Currently only the literal `currentInstance === model.instance` and mutex-count are grep-checked; the stop-before-close invariant (research §4 SIGSEGV guard) is review-only. In Phase 1 this is fine since code-reviewer inspects `releaseEngine`, but a one-line grep anchor would make the guarantee resilient to later edits.
findings.recommendation: Add a smoke step: `grep -nE 'stopResponse\(model\)[^}]*cleanUp\(model' core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/DefaultModelRegistry.kt` (multiline via `-U` if needed), or a weaker proxy: assert both `stopResponse(model)` and `cleanUp(model, onDone = { })` appear inside `releaseEngine`. Optional for round 1; can be absorbed into Task 10 smoke instead.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor section text is sufficient but does not cite the testability posture of the implementation — i.e. that every collaborator (`LlmModelHelper`, `DownloadRepository`, `AllowlistLoader`, `ErrorLog`) is already interface-typed and ctor-injected, so Phase-2 unit tests need no refactor. Making this explicit would pre-empt a future test-reviewer asking whether the deferral is accumulating hidden coupling debt.
findings.recommendation: Append one sentence to Task 6 TDD Anchor: "Testability posture: all external collaborators are interfaces injected via Hilt ctor, so Phase-2 JUnit5+MockK tests can be added without touching DefaultModelRegistry source." Zero code impact, resolves the debt-quality question cleanly.
